title: Install AIDE
triage_reason: Installing the AIDE package only adds a file integrity checking tool; it does not modify authentication, networking, or critical system files, and poses minimal risk of lockout or breakage.
triage_estimated_impact: Potential package conflict if AIDE is already installed via another repository; Minimal disk space usage

title: Build and Test AIDE Database
triage_reason: The remediation installs the AIDE package (if missing), initializes its database, and copies the new database file. These actions are standard package installation and file creation steps that do not alter authentication, networking, or critical system files, and pose minimal risk of lockout or system breakage.
triage_estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Temporary disk usage during database generation; Potential overwrite of an existing /var/lib/aide/aide.db.gz file

title: Configure AIDE to Verify the Audit Tools
triage_reason: The remediation installs the AIDE package (if missing) and adds/updates lines in /etc/aide.conf to include audit tools with integrity checks. This is a straightforward package install and config file edit that does not affect authentication, networking, or critical services, and poses minimal risk of system breakage or lockout.
triage_estimated_impact: Potential syntax error in /etc/aide.conf if edit fails, causing AIDE to fail on next run
review_concerns: The plan uses a simple substring match (grep -q) to check for the path in /etc/aide.conf, which may unintentionally replace lines containing the path as a substring (e.g., in comments or unrelated paths), potentially breaking existing configuration.; The plan omits the ausearch tool (/usr/sbin/ausearch), which was included in the original remediation script and may be required for complete audit tool integrity checking.
qa_verdict_reason: The plan correctly installs AIDE if missing, ensures the configuration file exists, and updates or adds integrity rules for the audit tools auditctl and auditd. These changes are configuration-only and unlikely to disrupt services or cause lockouts. The omission of ausearch is a minor completeness issue but does not introduce risk.

title: Enable Dracut FIPS Module
triage_reason: The finding describes a missing kernel boot parameter (fips=1) required to enable FIPS mode via dracut. This is a configuration issue that can be remediated by adjusting the kernel command line and dracut configuration, after which a reboot is needed.
triage_estimated_impact: reboot required; FIPS mode not enabled; System may not meet FIPS compliance

title: Enable FIPS Mode
triage_reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires the kernel boot parameter fips=1 and cannot be applied to a running system; attempting to enable FIPS mode on an installed system is unsupported and may render the system unbootable or break cryptographic services.
triage_estimated_impact: reboot required; system may become unbootable; loss of SSH/console access; cryptographic service failures

title: FIPS Must Use a Supported Subpolicy
triage_reason: Ensure that only FIPS-approved subpolicies are used to maintain cryptographic security and avoid weakening the system.
triage_estimated_impact: Use of non-approved subpolicies such as NO-ENFORCE-EMS; Failure to restrict AD-SUPPORT to operationally required scenarios

It does not alter authentication mechanisms, services, or boot configuration, and only affects password validation at next change, posing minimal risk of lockout.
triage_estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Existing passwords lacking uppercase characters will fail complexity checks on next password change; If applied to service or system accounts with static passwords, authentication could fail

triage_estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; debug-shell service enabled; unauthorized root shell access; tty9 access

title: Disable Ctrl-Alt-Del Burst Action

title: Set Interactive Session Timeout
triage_reason: Configuring TMOUT ensures inactive user sessions are automatically terminated, reducing the risk of unattended sessions being left open.

title: Prevent remote hosts from connecting to the proxy display
triage_reason: The SSH daemon should prevent remote hosts from connecting to the proxy display. The default SSH configuration for X11UseLocalhost is yes, which prevents remote hosts from connecting to the proxy display. To explicitly prevent remote connections to the proxy display, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: X11UseLocalhost yes.
triage_estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; networking affected; SSH misconfiguration; Unauthorized remote access; Proxy exposure

attempt_2_commands: cp /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf.bak; mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d; sed -i '/^\s*X11UseLocalhost\s*/s/.*/X11UseLocalhost yes/I' /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf; echo 'X11UseLocalhost yes' >> /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf; sshd -t; systemctl restart sshd
